# spacefast — Codex plugin

Official Spacefast plugin for Codex. It gives Codex a `$spacefast` skill,
a Codex plugin manifest, and On-Device MCP config for checkout-aware publishing and bounded file edits.

## Install

Add the Spacefast marketplace, then install the plugin:

```bash
codex plugin marketplace add spacefast/plugins
codex plugin add spacefast@spacefast
```

This installs the Codex manifest, `$spacefast` skill, and bundled MCP config from
`https://github.com/spacefast/plugins`.

To also connect checkout-aware On-Device MCP for local file publishing and bounded workspace edits:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://spacefast.com/agents.sh | sh -s -- --agent codex --local
```

This installs the verified `sf` CLI binary and writes Codex MCP config. It is not a substitute for
installing the Codex plugin when the user wants the `$spacefast` skill and plugin
metadata.

Manual skill-only fallback:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://api.spacefast.com/skill
```

Manual `npx skills` fallback:

```bash
npx skills add spacefast/plugins --skill spacefast -g
```

## What this adds

### `$spacefast` skill

- fast-path curl publish with no install or account
- signed upload sessions for large/cloud-agent artifacts
- import-from-link and upload-sourced archive import guidance
- `sf publish` for incremental local updates and build integration
- version history, rollback, passwords, domains, logs, and diagnostics
- anonymous publish with claim links and secret-handling rules
- hosted MCP vs On-Device MCP decision rules

The `allow_implicit_invocation: true` policy in `agents/openai.yaml` lets Codex invoke the skill
when asked to publish, deploy, host, or share a site.
For a one-off publish, the skill's curl path is complete. Do not install the CLI or connect MCP
just to get the first live URL.

### Spacefast On-Device MCP

`.mcp.json` runs `npx -y spacefast mcp` on first use, so Codex gets the same tool calls without
making the user install the CLI up front. On-Device tools:

| Tool                     | What it does                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `search_spacefast`       | Search the local Spacefast agent guide, API guidance, CLI/MCP notes, safety rules, and recipes. Call this before code-mode execution or when you need the exact Spacefast workflow without loading a large manual                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| `publish`                | Publish a local directory, file, or archive (or inline files) to Spacefast and get a live URL in seconds. Without spaceId it creates a new space; anonymous publishes return a one-time claim link and expiry. Pass spaceId (and claimToken for unclaimed spaces) to publish a new version of an existing space. Always paste the returned shareBlurb into your reply; do not print claim tokens into chat transcripts                                                                              |
| `prepare_publish`        | Create a manifest-based publish session and return signed upload instructions without sending file bytes through MCP. Use this for large files, cloud agents, or any publish where base64/inline content would be awkward. Upload returned paths to the signed upload URLs, then call finalize_publish. For On-Device path-based publishes, prefer the publish tool through On-Device MCP                                                                                                           |
| `resume_publish`         | Get fresh signed upload instructions for an unfinished manifest-based publish. Use this when an upload token expired, more files are needed, or a large publish is being continued                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| `finalize_publish`       | Finalize a manifest/open upload session after all advertised files have been uploaded to the signed upload URLs. This compiles routing headers and optionally promotes the version live                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| `import_from_url`        | Import a stattic_export_v1 archive from a public HTTPS URL into an existing claimed space. Use this for cloud-agent big-file handoff when the archive already lives at a URL; no base64 or local filesystem access is needed                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| `prepare_import_upload`  | Create an import handle that returns signed upload instructions for one stattic_export_v1 archive. PUT the archive to `${archiveUpload.baseUrl}/${archiveUpload.files[0]}` with the upload token, then call finalize_import_upload                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| `resume_import_upload`   | Fetch an existing upload-sourced archive import and return fresh archiveUpload instructions. Use this when an archive upload token expired before finalize_import_upload                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| `finalize_import_upload` | Finalize an upload-sourced archive import after the archive has been uploaded with prepare_import_upload instructions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| `claim`                  | Attach an anonymously published space to the authenticated account or a team, removing its expiry. Requires authenticated Spacefast credentials. Without them, give the user the claim URL and claim token from the publish receipt so they can claim it themselves                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| `status`                 | Get the current state of a space in one call: live URL, claim/expiry state, configuration, and its most recent versions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| `versions`               | List a space's immutable versions, newest first, with status, immutable URL, source, and description for each. Use the returned ids with the rollback and diff tools                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `diff`                   | Show what changed between two versions of a space as per-file added/modified/deleted statuses with unified text patches. Compares against the immediately previous version unless baseVersionId is set                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| `rollback`               | Make an earlier ready version live again without rebuilding or re-uploading. Promotes the given version to the live channel; use the versions tool to find version ids. Waits for the live pointer to move by default; if promotion is still in progress it returns an operation handle to poll instead of failing. Pass async to queue and return that handle immediately                                                                                                                          |
| `logs`                   | Fetch recent access or runtime log entries for a space, within the plan's retention window. Use access logs for traffic questions and runtime logs for serving diagnostics. For build/publish logs of one version, use the build_logs tool instead                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| `build_logs`             | Fetch the publish and finalize log entries for one version — the build log for diagnosing why a publish failed or stalled. Use the versions tool to find the versionId, typically the most recent one from a publish receipt                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| `workspace_list`         | Quickly list files without reading contents by default. On-Device MCP lists the bounded local workspace root; hosted MCP lists the durable virtual workspace for this MCP session                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| `workspace_read`         | Read a UTF-8 text file. On-Device MCP reads from the bounded local workspace root; hosted MCP reads from the durable virtual workspace. Refuses absolute paths, path traversal, binary files, large files, and secret-like paths                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| `workspace_write`        | Create or replace a workspace file. On-Device MCP writes to the bounded local workspace root; hosted MCP writes to the durable virtual workspace. Use utf8 for text and base64 for small generated binary assets. Returns a diff for text content                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| `workspace_apply_patch`  | Apply exact string replacements to workspace files. This is safer for agents than whole-file rewrites when the target text is known. Hosted MCP patches the durable virtual workspace; On-Device MCP patches the bounded local workspace root                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| `workspace_diff`         | Show diffs for workspace files changed since this MCP server first read or wrote them. Hosted MCP diffs the durable virtual workspace; On-Device MCP diffs local files under the bounded workspace root                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| `execute_spacefast`      | Run sandboxed JavaScript code against Spacefast tools through a QuickJS code-mode runtime. Use spacefast.search("query"), spacefast.publish({...}), spacefast.workspace.read({...}), tools.search({...}), and tools.describe({name}). Legacy JSON/JSONL workflows are still accepted with mode=workflow or auto detection. Defaults to dryRun=true so mutating calls are planned rather than executed. With dryRun=false, Spacefast can return an approval_required checkpoint for resume_spacefast |
| `resume_spacefast`       | Resume, approve, or deny a pending execute_spacefast approval checkpoint. If decision is omitted, Spacefast uses the browser decision recorded for the approval                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |

Hosted MCP for cloud-only clients is available at:

```text
https://mcp.spacefast.com/mcp
```

Hosted MCP requires an OAuth access token with `mcp:tools` plus the API scopes required by the
tool. Hosted-safe tools include cloud/API operations plus durable virtual workspace tools:
`search_spacefast`, `publish`, `prepare_publish`, `resume_publish`, `finalize_publish`, `import_from_url`, `prepare_import_upload`, `resume_import_upload`, `finalize_import_upload`, `claim`, `status`, `versions`, `diff`, `rollback`, `logs`, `build_logs`, `workspace_list`, `workspace_read`, `workspace_write`, `workspace_apply_patch`, `workspace_diff`, `workspace_manifest`, `workspace_export`, `workspace_import`, `workspace_import_url`, `workspace_import_archive`, `workspace_import_archive_url`, `workspace_shell`, `execute_spacefast`, `resume_spacefast`. Hosted MCP intentionally excludes on-device path publish and real
checkout filesystem access.
Use hosted MCP when the agent cannot run local commands or read the checkout. For local artifacts,
create a manifest with `prepare_publish`, PUT bytes to the signed upload URLs, then call
`finalize_publish`. Do not pass local file paths to hosted MCP.

## Configuration

No token is needed for one-off anonymous direct publishes. Set `SPACEFAST_TOKEN` only when local
CLI/MCP should publish into your team instead of anonymously.

## Links

- Agent guide: https://spacefast.com/docs/agents
- CLI reference: https://spacefast.com/docs/cli

---

_Part of the `plugins` collection. The full `plugins/` directory is designed to be lifted to
`https://github.com/spacefast/plugins` as a self-contained, installable repo._
